mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above the 0.2 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings declines, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production above its long-term trend reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO trend deviations show negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, or rolling IC drops below 0.01 over 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with accommodative monetary policy creates a dual momentum effect where labor market tightness supports wage growth and low rates sustain valuation multiples, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below the 1.5 percent quarterly threshold indicates severe demand contraction and recession risk, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or the threshold model fails to outperform a baseline mean-reversion strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and force central banks to tighten policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through margin compression and higher discount rates.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show positive correlation with 60-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above the 104.0 index threshold signals robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 104.0 fail to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold signal shows negative predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.
